steps.title: Identify the three-item series
steps.content: The sentence lists three pieces of data being shown: length (62 km), slip rate (between 0.2 and 1.0 mm/yr), and most recent deformation (less than 15 thousand years ago). In Standard English, a three-item series requires proper punctuation to separate items and parenthetical details must attach directly to their nouns.

steps.title: Apply the correct series punctuation
steps.content: Choice C correctly keeps parenthetical details attached directly to their nouns—length (62 km), slip rate (between 0.2 and 1.0 mm/yr)—then uses commas to separate all three series items before and most recent deformation. Choices A, B, and D all violate series punctuation: A wrongly substitutes semicolons for commas, B inserts commas between nouns and their parenthetical details, and D omits the comma between the first two items. That matches choice C — C is correct.
